```json
  "findings": [
    "Thermal growth offset was not incorporated into the shaft alignment procedure for a hot-service rotating machine.",
    "Cold alignment was performed without accounting for expected thermal expansion of the driver and driven equipment, leading to misalignment under operating temperature.",
    "Likely symptoms include elevated vibration, bearing overheating, coupling wear, and premature seal or bearing failures."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Stop or schedule shutdown to re-evaluate alignment with thermal growth offsets per OEM and API 686 guidance.",
    "Calculate or obtain thermal growth values for both driver and driven machines at operating temperature.",
    "Perform hot alignment verification using laser alignment tools with thermal offset targets applied at cold condition.",
    "Document final alignment values and update maintenance records."
  ],
  "rationale": "API 686 and laser alignment manuals require that thermal growth be considered when aligning shafts in hot service. Ignoring thermal offsets means the machine will run misaligned once at operating temperature, causing vibration, bearing damage, coupling wear, and potential failure. Because the equipment is in service and the condition is non-compliant with accepted alignment practice, the decision is fail with high risk.",
  "references": [
    "API 686 - Recommended Practice for Machinery Installation and Installation Design",
    "Laser shaft alignment system manufacturer manuals"
  ]
}
```
